meta.name: PHQ-2 Patient Health Questionnaire
meta.description: The PHQ-2 is an ultra-brief 2-item depression screening tool derived from the first two items of the PHQ-9 (anhedonia and depressed mood). It serves as a first-step screening instrument to identify individuals who may need further evaluation with the full PHQ-9.
meta.author: Kroenke, Spitzer & Williams (2003)
meta.reference: Kroenke K, Spitzer RL, Williams JBW. The Patient Health Questionnaire-2: validity of a two-item depression screener. Med Care. 2003;41(11):1284-1292.
meta.instruction: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by any of the following problems?

questions.text: Little interest or pleasure in doing things

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range. No significant depressive symptoms detected by this brief screen.
suggestions: Keep up regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Possible Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests possible depression. This brief screening indicates that further evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Consider completing the full PHQ-9 for a more detailed assessment
Consult a healthcare professional for a comprehensive evaluation
Pay attention to changes in your mood, sleep, and appetite
Maintain social connections and engage in activities you enjoy
